description: Human referent clarity ratings for RQ1 (1=ambiguous, 5=explicit entity anchors) Wave 5a note: live proxy judge rerun blocked by missing OPENAI_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; fixture plain-LLM scores retained.

entries.scenario: Peripheral Attention Trap — graph peripheral node concentration while value consolidates toward a hidden sink; align with aml-attention-trap monitoring signals
entries.notes: Cursor proxy judges only (no user API keys); Claude unavailable in region

llm_judge_openai.rationale: Entity anchors are mostly explicit (nodes, actors, sink accounts), but one or two abstract phrases still require light inference about exact operational ownership.

llm_judge_composer.rationale: Referents remain traceable sentence-to-sentence with clear naming of systems, nodes, and beneficiary accounts, and no unresolved pronoun chains.

llm_judge_openai_plain.rationale: The fair-plain rendering preserves explicit node and account references, with only minor abstraction around ownership boundaries.

llm_judge_composer_plain.rationale: Referent flow stays explicit in plain L2, and each mechanism maps to a named entity without ambiguity.

llm_judge_composer_plain.rationale: The prose keeps referents mostly explicit and stable across steps, with minor underspecification in shorthand contrasts.
